rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when necessary.
generic_rules: Only reference AADL features or helpers that are explicitly visible in the current scope.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that depend on package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Do not introduce Boolean-to-integer conversions in AGREE contracts unless the data type is explicitly numeric.
Validate that all AGREE expressions have valid types before generating guarantees or assumptions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees over visible ports; implementation annexes are for subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes without proper scope validation.
Do not generate AGREE clauses that reference undefined or opaque data types without explicit scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
